summary: Justin Brennan teaches how to buy 10-30 unit multifamily apartments with $20,000 out-of-pocket capital by leveraging OPM (other people's money) through syndication, obtaining 65-70% bank financing, and capturing acquisition fees (2-3%) that effectively reduce initial capital requirements. The core strategy involves positioning yourself as the General Partner bringing 10% of capital while raising 90% from investors, earning 30-40% equity plus acquisition fees and asset management fees.
takeaways: Buy 15-unit deals at $50k-$150k per unit in non-coastal markets (Midwest, South, East Coast but not CA/NY/coastal)
Acquire property for $1.5M + $100k renovations + $50k closing costs = $1.65M all-in, finance 70% ($1.15M loan), need $500k down payment
GP brings 10% of down payment (~$50k), receives 30-40% of profits plus 2-3% acquisition fee (~$30k), resulting in $20k true out-of-pocket
$1.25 trillion in commercial loans due 2025-2026 creates stressed seller opportunities for discounted deals
Find deals via direct-to-broker relationships (pre-market/off-market) and direct-to-owner software targeting 150-160M properties
Class B properties (1980-2005 vintage with some amenities) outperform Class A and C during recessions and normal markets due to consistent occupancy
Bank underwrites property financials/cashflow, NOT personal credit/income—600+ credit score sufficient; property must substantiate the loan

skill_candidates.change: Master the 10-part financial breakdown: purchase price, renovations, closing costs, loan amount (70% LTV), down payment structure, GP equity split (10% capital, 30-40% profits), and acquisition fees (2-3%)
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin showed exact 15-unit example ($1.5M purchase + $100k reno + $50k closing = $1.65M all-in) and walked through how $50k down payment with $30k acquisition fee = $20k net out-of-pocket; emphasized deal analyzers in toolbox to replicate this model

skill_candidates.change: Build direct relationships with 3+ brokers in target market within one trip; request both on-market AND off-market deals (60%+ of inventory is pre-market)
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin's Kansas City case study: visited three properties from Crexy, built rapport with brokers, accessed property managers and construction crews through broker referrals in one 3-day trip; stated 'for every deal on website, I have three that are not'

skill_candidates.change: Position yourself as GP contributing 10% of down payment to control 30-40% of deal profits; script the value prop: 'If this deal returns 15-18-20% annually with $25k minimum, who do you know that might be interested?'
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin demonstrated that good deals attract capital ('money will chase good deals'); showed 31-unit example where he raised $580k from 7 investors + seller financing for $645k down payment by proving the deal's cash flow; stated 'if your deal is good, raising money is not hard'

skill_candidates.change: Execute 90-day action plan: week-by-week accountability checklist to move from prospect to closed deal within 90 days using provided 90-day guide and weekly accountability coaching
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin emphasized 'getting to the edge of the cliff after you learn it and making the house show up' and included 90-day action guide with weekly checkpoints in mentorship program; highlighted Shai's success with accountability coaching removing excuses

title: Multifamily Syndication Explained: Buy Apartments in 90 Days Using Other People’s Money
